text: Detection proposal methods have become the de facto standard for object detection. We investigate the design decisions that are made using these proposal methods and their relative merits. We analyze the performance of 12 popular region proposal methods on 4 datasets along with 4 baseline methods. We analyze the repeatability, ground truth coverage, and the performance of Detection Proposal Network (DPM), R-CNN, and Fast R-CNN. Our results show that for object detection, improving localization accuracy of the proposal is as important as improving the recall of the proposals. We also introduce a novel metric, average recall (AR), to measure the balance between recall and localization accuracy of the proposals, and surprisingly, correlates highly with the accuracy of object detection models. Overall, our work gives an overview of the strengths and weaknesses of the widely used region proposal methods, as well as the metrics to use when choosing or designing proposal methods.
explanation: The text reads like coherent academic prose with domain-specific terminology. The reference to 'Detection Proposal Network (DPM)' alongside R-CNN variants could indicate a factual mix-up often seen in AI-generated content.
